sizing_rule.note: Ralph Vince optimal f method: Position Size = Account Stake / (Largest Historical Loss / Optimal f). Optimal f is calculated from historical trade results; example parameters show f values of 0.24 vs 0.16. Dollars per unit = Largest Loss / Optimal f; units to trade = account stake / dollars per unit.

source_rule_text: Position size is determined by dividing your total account stake by the largest loss divided by the optimal f value, with position units allocated proportionally to account size.
